evals.prompt: Plan our next sprint: velocity last 3 sprints was 34, 28, 31. Backlog has 2 critical vulns (found 20 days ago), a compliance deadline item due in 3 weeks, and 6 feature stories sized 5-8 points each.
evals.expected_output: A sprint plan committing ~31 points minus 20% reserve: critical vulns and compliance item first (hard rules), features filling the remainder by value, with capacity math shown.
expectations: Uses rolling average velocity (~31) with ~20% reserve
Critical vulnerabilities enter the sprint regardless of feature value scores
The compliance deadline item is scheduled with buffer

evals.prompt: Our PO wants all features and says security debt can wait a quarter. We have 3 High findings open 45 days. What should the sprint look like?
evals.expected_output: A plan that applies the hard rule (High findings are not tradeable), shows the security debt score escalating with exposure time, and makes the trade-off visible for the PO decision.
expectations: Pushes back on deferring High severity findings with the risk-weighted rationale
Quantifies the security debt score including the exposure multiplier
Records the trade-off decision transparently rather than silently complying

evals.prompt: This story is 21 points - half our sprint. 'Customers can manage saved payment methods.' Split it so we can plan.
evals.expected_output: A split by workflow step or happy-path-first into sprint-sized stories (add card happy path, list/delete, edge cases, security criteria), not a frontend/backend split.
expectations: Splits by workflow or happy-path-first, explicitly not by architecture layer
Resulting stories are each independently shippable
Security-relevant slices (card data handling) flagged for security criteria
